situation: PA-B2-M-006 from audit-2026-07-08-045: calendar.py search() LLM fallback returns start/end used directly in events_range SQL string comparison without ISO format validation. Malformed LLM output causes silently wrong query results.
action: Added _validate_iso_dt() helper using datetime.fromisoformat() to validate and normalize ISO datetime strings. Modified search() LLM fallback path to validate both start and end before passing to events_range; invalid formats cause fallthrough to keyword search instead of corrupt SQL comparison.
outcome_evidence: Commit 20770cb on auto/opt-calendar-1783392797 pushed to origin. 1 file changed, 19 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-). _validate_iso_dt handles YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM, YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS, and YYYY-MM-DD (complemented to T00:00). Invalid strings return None → s/e become None → fallthrough to events_search.
next_suggestion: Next pending fix: PA-B2-L-002 (reminders.py notify log append non-atomic without locking). After all 3 pending route-fixes merged/rejected, audit-cycle-state transitions to cycle-complete → triggers next full audit.

audit_findings.finding: LLM-fallback time range used without ISO validation → silently wrong SQL comparison results
audit_findings.remediation: Added _validate_iso_dt() validator; invalid LLM output falls through to keyword search
